title: Podman fails to start container after update to 1.6.0
body:
After updating from 1.5.1 to 1.6.0, podman cannot start containers.
Downgrading back to 1.5.1, lets me start containers again.

Any help would be great to get 1.6.0 to work.
```sh
[petoem@notebook:~]$ uname -a                                                                                                                                                                             (10-03 17:29)
Linux notebook 5.3.1-arch1-1-ARCH #1 SMP PREEMPT Sat Sep 21 11:33:49 UTC 2019 x86_64 GNU/Linux

[petoem@notebook:~]$ podman version --log-level debug                                                                                                                                                     (10-03 17:30)

DEBU[0000] Initializing boltdb state at /home/petoem/.local/share/containers/storage/libpod/bolt_state.db
```

comments.body: Can you try `podman system migrate`?  I didn't think it was necessary for this release, but might have missed that proclamation.
This looks like a slirp4netns issue that was resolved in 1.6.1

@TomSweeneyRedHat I ran `podman system migrate` (output below), it sadly didn't help. 
But I noticed that whenever I reboot, the first command I run with podman panics and the subsequent commands  sometime display an error like this `ERRO[0000] Error refreshing container 13a1c14093fbe545bd39d7c8de6915c2cbd4303dcaaad4026e0f97c2c7ba5de7: error acquiring lock 0 for container 13a1c14093fbe545bd39d7c8de6915c2cbd4303dcaaad4026e0f97c2c7ba5de7: file exists`.
